xccdf:Benchmark.xccdf:title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8 Security Technical Implementation Guide

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:title: RHEL 8 must enable the SELinux targeted policy.
xccdf:Rule.xccdf:description: Without verification of the security functions, security functions may not operate correctly and the failure may go unnoticed.
xccdf:Rule.xccdf:fixtext: Configure RHEL 8 to verify the SELinux targeted policy in /etc/selinux/config.

xccdf:check.xccdf:check-content: Verify the SELinux targeted policy: sestatus | grep "Loaded policy"

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:title: RHEL 8 must ensure the SSH server is configured to use only FIPS-validated key exchange algorithms.
xccdf:Rule.xccdf:description: Unapproved mechanisms used for authentication to the cryptographic module are not verified and cannot be relied upon.
xccdf:Rule.xccdf:fixtext: Configure the SSH server to use FIPS-validated key exchange algorithms in /etc/crypto-policies.

xccdf:Rule.xccdf:title: RHEL 8 must display the Standard Mandatory DoD Notice and Consent Banner before granting local access.
xccdf:Rule.xccdf:description: Display of the DoD consent banner before granting access ensures privacy and security notification.
xccdf:Rule.xccdf:fixtext: Populate /etc/issue with the Standard Mandatory DoD Notice and Consent Banner.
